title: aspidogyne pedicellata
intro: Aspidogyne pedicellata, also known as the "Galeola orchid", is a rare and endemic species of orchid native to Sri Lanka and is characterized by its unique, small flowers and pedicellate leaves. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Aspidogyne pedicellata include its potential use in treating various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Aspidogyne pedicellata, including infusions and decoctions, are traditionally used in Sri Lankan medicine to promote overall well-being and to treat various ailments.
